name: Russian Civil War
description: Bolsheviks vs White Army, foreign interventionists, and everyone else. War Communism, grain requisitioning, and general chaos.

name: Polish-Soviet War
description: Tukhachevsky's march on Warsaw ends in the "Miracle on the Vistula." Minor resource drain, major embarrassment.

name: Winter War
description: Finland declines Soviet territorial demands. The Red Army discovers that quantity does not always compensate for Finnish snipers and -40C.

name: Great Patriotic War
description: Operation Barbarossa. 27 million dead. Total mobilization of economy, workforce, and spirit. The defining crisis of the Soviet century.

name: Korean War
description: Proxy war support for North Korea. Material aid and "volunteer" pilots drain resources, but the fighting stays far from home.

name: Hungarian Uprising
description: Budapest rises. Soviet tanks restore order. Brief but clarifying: the limits of de-Stalinization are defined in T-54 caliber.

name: Prague Spring Intervention
description: Czechoslovakia attempts "socialism with a human face." Moscow decides the face should remain stern. Warsaw Pact intervenes.

name: Soviet-Afghan War
description: The Soviet Vietnam. A decade of casualties, demoralization, and mounting costs that erode public faith in the system.

name: Russian Famine of 1921-22
description: Post-civil-war collapse meets drought. 5 million dead. ARA (American Relief Administration) barely dents the catastrophe.

name: Holodomor
description: Forced grain confiscation during collectivization. 3.5-7.5 million dead in Ukraine alone. Internal passports prevent escape.

name: Post-War Famine of 1946-47
description: Drought meets wartime exhaustion. 1-1.5 million dead. State continues grain exports despite domestic starvation.

name: Kyshtym Nuclear Disaster
description: Nuclear waste tank explosion at Mayak. INES Level 6. Entire villages evacuated. Classified for decades. The first major Soviet nuclear accident.

name: Nedelin Catastrophe
description: R-16 ICBM explodes on the launch pad at Baikonur. 78 dead including Marshal Nedelin. Hushed up — officially he died in a plane crash.

name: Chernobyl Disaster
description: Reactor No. 4 explodes. The invisible enemy. Liquidators, exclusion zones, and a crack in Soviet invincibility that never heals.

name: Aral Sea Crisis
description: Cotton irrigation diverts the Amu Darya and Syr Darya. The Aral Sea shrinks to 10% of its volume. Fishing villages become desert outposts.

name: Armenian Earthquake
description: Spitak earthquake, magnitude 6.8. 25,000 dead. Soviet concrete-panel construction collapses like cards. Gorbachev cuts short his UN trip.

name: Red Terror
description: Cheka unleashed after Fanny Kaplan's assassination attempt on Lenin. Mass executions, hostage-taking, and concentration camps. "Better to arrest a hundred innocent than let one guilty escape."

name: Great Terror (Yezhovshchina)
description: Stalin's purges consume the Party, the military, the intelligentsia. 750,000 executed, 1.3 million sent to the Gulag. Trust becomes extinct.

name: De-Stalinization
description: Khrushchev's Secret Speech denounces Stalin's cult of personality. Political prisoners released, censorship relaxed. A brief, bewildering thaw.

name: Virgin Lands Campaign
description: Khrushchev's gamble: plow the Kazakh steppe for grain. Initial bumper harvests give way to dust bowls. The soil was not consulted.

name: Anti-Alcohol Campaign
description: Gorbachev restricts vodka production and sales. State revenue collapses. Citizens pivot to samogon (moonshine), sugar disappears from stores.

name: Glasnost & Perestroika
description: Gorbachev attempts to reform the unreformable. Openness reveals the rot; restructuring dismantles what remains. The cure proves terminal.

name: Kronstadt Rebellion
description: Sailors at Kronstadt — heroes of the Revolution — demand free soviets. Trotsky sends the Red Army across the ice. The Revolution devours its own.

name: Doctors' Plot
description: Stalin accuses Jewish doctors of poisoning Kremlin leaders. A new purge looms — then Stalin dies in March 1953. The plot dies with him.

name: Sino-Soviet Split
description: The Communist bloc fractures. China and the USSR compete for ideological supremacy. Border skirmishes on the Ussuri River follow.

name: Cuban Missile Crisis
description: The world teeters on the brink of nuclear war. Moscow demands maximum military readiness from all settlements. For thirteen days in October, every citizen lives under the shadow of annihilation.

name: August Coup
description: Hardliners attempt to overthrow Gorbachev. Yeltsin stands on a tank. Three days later the coup collapses — and the Union follows.
